challenges.title: MailMessage Class
challenges.description: Create a `MailMessage` class with attributes: subject, sender, body, and date. The constructor should accept all four as arguments. Implement `__str__` to return 'From: {sender} | Subject: {subject}'. Implement `summary(max_body=50)` that returns the first `max_body` characters of the body followed by '...' if truncated.
challenges.test_code:
msg = MailMessage('Hello', 'alice@test.com', 'This is the body of the email.', '2026-01-01')
assert msg.subject == 'Hello'
assert msg.sender == 'alice@test.com'
assert msg.body == 'This is the body of the email.'
assert msg.date == '2026-01-01'
assert str(msg) == 'From: alice@test.com | Subject: Hello'
assert msg.summary() == 'This is the body of the email.'
long_body = 'A' * 100
msg2 = MailMessage('Test', 'bob@test.com', long_body, '2026-03-15')
summary = msg2.summary()
assert len(summary) == 53
assert summary.endswith('...')
assert msg2.summary(10) == 'AAAAAAAAAA...'
short = MailMessage('Hi', 'x@y.com', 'Short', '2026-01-01')
assert short.summary(50) == 'Short'
hints: Store all four parameters as instance attributes in __init__.
In summary(), check if len(self.body) > max_body. If so, slice and add '...'.

challenges.title: Threaded Mail Fetcher
challenges.description: Write a `MailFetcher` class that simulates fetching mail with status tracking. It should have: a `status` attribute (starts as 'idle'), a `messages` list (starts empty), a `fetch(message_list)` method that sets status to 'fetching', stores the messages, and sets status to 'done', and a `get_results()` method that returns messages if status is 'done' or raises RuntimeError if not. Also implement `reset()` to go back to 'idle' with empty messages.
challenges.starter_code:
class MailFetcher:
    """Simulates an async mail fetcher with status tracking."""

    def __init__(self):
        # Your code here
        pass

    def fetch(self, message_list):
        """Simulate fetching messages. Updates status and stores results."""
        # Your code here
        pass

    def get_results(self):
        """Return fetched messages if done, else raise RuntimeError."""
        # Your code here
        pass

    def reset(self):
        """Reset to idle state with no messages."""
        # Your code here
        pass
challenges.test_code:
fetcher = MailFetcher()
assert fetcher.status == 'idle'
assert fetcher.messages == []
try:
    fetcher.get_results()
    assert False, 'Should have raised RuntimeError'
except RuntimeError:
    pass
test_msgs = ['msg1', 'msg2', 'msg3']
fetcher.fetch(test_msgs)
assert fetcher.status == 'done'
assert fetcher.get_results() == ['msg1', 'msg2', 'msg3']
fetcher.reset()
assert fetcher.status == 'idle'
assert fetcher.messages == []
try:
    fetcher.get_results()
    assert False, 'Should have raised RuntimeError after reset'
except RuntimeError:
    pass
fetcher.fetch([])
assert fetcher.status == 'done'
assert fetcher.get_results() == []
fetcher.fetch(['only_one'])
assert fetcher.get_results() == ['only_one']
hints: In get_results(), check if self.status == 'done' before returning messages.
